source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, lines AB and CD intersect at point E, with EF$\perp$AB at point E. If $\angle FEC - \angle AEC = 20^\circ$, what is the measure of $\angle AED$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Let $\angle AEC$ be $x$, then $\angle FEC=x+20^\circ$. \\
Since EF$\perp$AB, \\
it follows that $\angle AEF=90^\circ$, \\
which means $\angle AEC+\angle FEC= 90^\circ$, \\
hence $x+x+20^\circ=90^\circ$, \\
Solving this gives: $x=35^\circ$, \\
that is $\angle AEC=35^\circ$, \\
Therefore, $\angle AED=180^\circ−35^\circ=\boxed{145^\circ}$.
